name: German Shepherd

dietaryNeeds.breedSpecificNotes: German Shepherds have notoriously sensitive stomachs. Exocrine pancreatic insufficiency (EPI) is more common in this breed than any other, causing the pancreas to fail at producing digestive enzymes. They're also highly prone to bloat — feeding smaller, more frequent meals and avoiding exercise after eating is essential. Their deep chest and active nature mean they burn through calories fast, but quality matters more than quantity.

bestOverall.why: 80% meat with six protein sources delivers exceptional nutrition for this demanding breed. Low-temperature preparation preserves digestive enzymes that GSDs desperately need.

bestForSensitiveStomach.why: Single protein source eliminates common triggers. Turkey is gentle on the GSD's notoriously sensitive digestive system, and the moderate fat content won't overwhelm the pancreas.

bestForJoints.why: Built-in glucosamine and MSM support the breed's vulnerable hips and elbows. 60% meat provides quality protein for maintaining the muscle mass that protects joints.

bestValue.why: Cold-pressed for better digestibility — crucial for a breed prone to EPI. Lower fat at 12% reduces pancreatic strain. Excellent price point for a large breed.

bestPremium.why: 85% animal ingredients with WholePrey ratios including organs. The closest thing to a biologically appropriate diet for a breed that thrives on nutrient density.

name: Dachshund

keyNutrients: calcium and phosphorus for spine health
dietaryNeeds.breedSpecificNotes: Weight management is the single most important dietary factor for Dachshunds. Their elongated spine makes them extremely vulnerable to intervertebral disc disease (IVDD), and every extra gram of weight increases spinal pressure. A lean Dachshund is a healthy Dachshund — you should always be able to feel their ribs. Low-fat, high-protein food keeps muscle supporting the spine without adding dangerous weight. Small kibble size matters too — Dachshunds have small mouths and can struggle with large biscuits.

bestOverall.why: Moderate protein and fat ratio perfect for a breed where weight control is critical. Small kibble size suits their smaller mouths. Natural ingredients with no fillers that cause weight gain.

bestForWeightControl.why: Lowest fat on our list at 12% — ideal for a breed where every gram counts. Cold-pressed for slow energy release that prevents the sugar spikes leading to overeating.

bestForSpineHealth.why: Glucosamine and MSM support the discs and joints under constant strain. Quality protein maintains the muscle mass that protects an elongated spine.

bestValue.why: Single protein with moderate fat. At £6.33/kg, Dachshunds eat so little that even premium food is affordable — a 6kg bag lasts over a month.

bestPremium.why: Balanced 60% meat formula with moderate calories. Multiple proteins for nutritional variety without the extreme calorie density of Orijen.
